page: Microsoft Azure E32-16ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. It also includes 1229 GB of local SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring in the top 25% at 2703.79. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range. Specifically, stress-ng multi-core performance scored 21625.7, while read-only large block memory bandwidth reached 43.86 GB/sec. Database and web serving operations also align with average tier expectations, with Redis SET operations achieving 16.04 million operations per second and static web serving throughput measured at 17.38 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers high memory capacity relative to its compute allocation, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The primary tradeoff is the average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance despite the large memory footprint. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational database management systems, caching layers such as Redis, and moderate-scale data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a substantial 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding 16.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a 1229 GB SSD, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. This resource configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads that require a large memory footprint without requiring proportional scaling of compute cores. It is suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core CPU performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ads_v5 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local SSD storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB per vCPU
